about: Punjab National Bank is India's first Swadeshi Bank. PNB is a Banking and Financial service bank owned by the Government of India with its headquarters is in New Delhi, India. It is the Second largest PSU after SBI.[1]

key_points.raw:
ABOUT [ edit ]

Punjab National Bank is India's first Swadeshi Bank. PNB is a Banking and Financial service bank owned by the Government of India with its headquarters is in New Delhi, India. It is the Second largest PSU after SBI.[1]

KEY POINTS [ edit ]

Market Position[1]
The bank is among the top 3 Public Sector Banks in India and 2nd largest by way of business size.

Business Segments
Corporate/Wholesale Banking: 38% in Q3 FY26 vs 35% in FY22
Treasury: 29% in Q3 FY26 vs 34% in FY22
Retail Banking: 30% in Q3 FY26 vs 28% in FY22
Others: 3% in Q3 FY26 vs 3% in FY22 [2][3]

Key Ratios
GNPA: 3.19% in Q3 FY26 vs 11.78% in FY22
NNPA: 0.32% in Q3 FY26 vs 4.80% in FY22
PCR: 96.99% in Q3 FY26 vs 81.60% in FY22
CRAR 16.77% in Q3 FY26 vs 14.50% in FY22 [4][5]
Yield on Advances: 7.69% in Q3 FY26 vs 6.79% in FY22
Cost of Deposits: 5.09% in Q3 FY26 vs 3.99% in FY22
NIM: 2.52% in Q3 FY26 vs 2.71% in FY22[6][7]

Distribution Network
Total Domestic Branches: 10,261 in Q3 FY26 vs 10,098 in FY22
ATMs: 11,109 in Q3 FY26 vs 13,350 in FY22
Business Correspondents: 32,809 in Q3 FY26 vs 15,719 in FY22 [8][9]

Branch Mix:[8]
Rural: 38%
Semi-Urban: 25%
Urban: 20%
Metro: 17%

Advances[10]
As of Q3 FY26, the total global advances stood at Rs. 12,31,238 Cr vs Rs 11,10,292 Cr in Q3 FY25.
Advances Mix:
Retail: 24%
Agriculture: 16%
MSME: 16%
Corporate & Others: 43%

Deposits[11]
As of Q3 FY26, the total global deposits stood at Rs. 16,60,290 Cr vs Rs. 15,29,699 Cr in Q3 FY25.
Deposits Mix:
Term Deposits: 63%
Savings Deposits: 32%
Current Deposits: 5%

International Business
The bank operates in 6 countries, with branches in Dubai and GIFT City, Gujarat, serving its international business. It also has 2 subsidiaries in London, UK, and Bhutan, a JV in Nepal, and representative offices in Myanmar and Bangladesh. [12]The bank's international business represents 4% of its total operations, contributing 5.5% to global advances and 3.9% to global deposits as of Q3 FY26.[13]

Digital Initiatives[14]
Digital transactions increased 25% YoY to 344 crore in Q3FY26 (vs 275 crore in Q3FY25), with digital channels accounting for 94.86% of total transactions. PNB One activated users rose 20% YoY to 250 lakh as of Dec-2025, while UPI transactions through the platform grew 12% YoY to 108 lakh. Adoption of new digital channels accelerated, with PNB One Biz activated users increasing to 2.42 lakh from 0.36 lakh YoY and WhatsApp banking users rising 81% YoY to 95.4 lakh as of Dec-2025.

Fundraise
It raised an equity capital of Rs 5,000 Cr through QIP in Q2 FY25. [15]

CEO Appointment
In Jan 2025, Shri Ashok Chandra was appointed Managing Director & Chief Executive Officer of the Bank. [16]

Management Guidance[17]
For FY26, management has guided for credit growth of 11-12% YoY, deposit growth of 9-10% YoY, CASA ratio above 38%, operating profit growth of 8-9% YoY, net interest income growth at ~7%. The bank aims to maintain NIM in the range of 2.8-2.9%. On asset quality, gross NPA is targeted below 3%, net NPA around 0.35%, and PCR (including TWO) above 96%. Credit cost is expected to remain below 0.5%, while annualized slippage ratio is guided below 1%. The bank has set a recovery target of ₹16,000 crore for FY26 and expects RoA to remain above 1%.
